title: Land Environment Working Group 2025
cpv_descriptions: 90700000 - Environmental services.

text:
ironment Working Group (LEWG) 2025 will be held Wednesday 25 June at Hotel Realm in Canberra.
The LEWG is Army's key engagement with Defence industry. The purpose of the event is to outline current and future land capability priorities and investment plans in a collaborative environment, enabling industry to provide early input and feedback into planning. Head Land Capability will co-chair the event with three industry Co-Chairs.
Registrations open 0900 AEST, Tuesday 25 March. Register at: australianarmy.eventsair.com/lewg-2025.
Other Instructions
**:** Day one of the LEWG 2025 is held at an UNCLASSIFIED level. On Thursday 26 June, classified briefings will be open to individuals who have attended the LEWG on 25 June and hold a Negative Vetting 1 Security Clearance or higher. Note, capacity is limited.
Conditions for Participation

cpv_descriptions: 90700000 - Environmental services.

Attendance is limited to two attendees per organisation. Registrations will be vetted to ensure the conditions of participation are adhered to.

Description
**:** The Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator (ASCA), through Mission Talon-Strike, is seeking technologies that deliver a capability edge to our Australian Defence Force (ADF) to solve the following Problem Statement:
title: Request for Tender for ASCA Mission Talon-Strike
cpv_descriptions: 38310000 - Precision balances.; 38422000 - Level-measuring equipment.; 50430000 - Repair and maintenance services of precision equipment.

text:
Talon-Strike, is seeking technologies that deliver a capability edge to our Australian Defence Force (ADF) to solve the following Problem Statement:
“How might we strike land targets from medium range with low cost, and launched loitering munitions?”
Mission Talon-Strike will accelerate Australian industry’s ability to deliver a low-cost, domestically manufactured medium-range Precision Loitering Munition (PLM) that bridges the short to long-range gap and provides the land combat force with enhanced operational effects to mitigate potential adversaries.
This procurement under Mission Talon-Strike is seeking suitable Flight Platform Vehicles, with associated Launcher, Transport and Storage System, to be delivered to Defence in suitable volumes to support PLM testing and certification activities.
Other Instructions
**:** Conditions for Participation
**:** Please refer to the Tender Details Schedule Conditions for Participation Clause 3.3.

) and intends to procure the HQJOC Expansion Project through Praeco under the existing PPP agreement.
To deliver the HQJOC Expansion Project, Praeco intends to procure a suitably qualified contractor to design, construct, commission and complete the required works (the
Project
**). The scope of the works will include, but is not limited to:** ·         An extension to the main operational building at the GJBC of approximately 17,700m (GBA) over two floors, including two large computer rooms and compartmented and open plan areas for approximately 400 Defence personnel.
·         A new collaboration and accommodation building of approximately 4,900m2 (GBA) over three floors, including office accommodation for Base Operations and Chaplaincy; a multifunction area for meetings, presentations and social/relaxation use; flexible collaboration spaces; and sleeping accommodation for up to 50 people.

·         Minor upgrade works to the existing mess facility and operational support building.
All work under the Project will have a heavy emphasis on security and will require significant contribution from the D&C Contractor during construction to enable the successful certification and accreditation of both physical spaces and information technology infrastructure.

Description
**:** The purpose of this notice is to invite you to attend an industry briefing regarding the RFT process for the provision of home loan services to eligible current and former Australian Defence Force (ADF) members accessing the Defence Home Ownership Assistance Scheme.
The Commonwealth is seeking to engage with interested providers to share our requirements, and gain industry insights into the Australian Home Loan Market.
**The services the Commonwealth will be seeking through the RFT include but not limited to:** a.     offering home loans and associated banking services to eligible ADF Members who meet the Scheme’s eligibility criteria, and manage approved loans for the term of the loan;
b.     offering eligible members competitive products and interest rates;

Description
**:** The Commonwealth, through the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD), is seeking specialist mentoring and software program services to deliver the ASD-sponsored Women in Security Mentoring Program (the Program). The Program will be delivered in partnership with the Australian Women in Security Network (AWSN) and ASD.
Other Instructions
title: Women in Security Mentoring Program
cpv_descriptions: 72212730 - Security software development services.; 80533100 - Computer training services.

text:
Program (the Program). The Program will be delivered in partnership with the Australian Women in Security Network (AWSN) and ASD.
Other Instructions
**:** The purpose of the Program is to attract, develop and retain women in the cyber security workforce in Australia by offering an online mentoring application that allows women in the industry to connect and grow their skills and confidence through mentoring with like-minded cyber security professionals. The Program offers networking, training resources, and ongoing support for participants’ mentoring journeys, aiming to deliver career pathways into the cyber industry and growing ASD’s workforce pipeline. The Program connects women at various stages in their careers with experienced mentors in cyber security, national intelligence and STEM industries.

ocurement to deliver a web mapping application that achieves Defence’s requirement to present UXO records to the public and maintain the application.

text:
Australia, as represented by the Department of Defence, is seeking to establish a Panel to provide domestic and international Air Transport services.
Strategic requirements under this Panel are likely to be in support of military Exercises, Operations and Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief within Australia and overseas. Services required under the Panel are anticipated to include provision of aircraft and movement of cargo and/or passengers.
A request for tender for the Air Transport Standing Offer for Services is anticipated to be released through AusTender in mid July 2025, or shortly thereafter.
Prospective tenderers are encouraged to review their notification settings in AusTender to ensure notification of further updates in relation to the RFT.

Description
**:** Defence Housing Australia (DHA) is issuing this Notice of Intended Procurement (NOIP) for our upcoming Brisbane - QLD New Builds Volume Leasing Program Expression of Interest (EOI).
Through this opportunity we are seeking to work with industry to deliver up to 380 newly constructed, high-quality properties to lease to DHA over the next 5 years across Brisbane, QLD.
Respondents who are unable to supply leased housing in the Brisbane region are eligible to participate in this procurement process if they are able to supply leased housing in other regions.
**The EOI provides an opportunity to develop and propose your own delivery model that suits your organisation, such as:** - creating and selling DHA-compliant house and land packages to investors with a DHA lease commitment*;
title: NOTICE OF INTENDED PROCUREMENT - SUPPLIER EOI FOR NEW BUILDS VOLUME LEASING PROGRAM IN BRISBANE, QLD
cpv_descriptions: 45000000 - Construction work.

text:
odel that suits your organisation, such as:** - creating and selling DHA-compliant house and land packages to investors with a DHA lease commitment*;
- coordinating the construction of new DHA-compliant properties on behalf of investors to lease to DHA*; and
- building or buying DHA-compliant properties to rent directly to DHA while retaining ownership.
*DHA will lease back the property if, on completion of construction of the property, DHA is satisfied that the property has been completed in accordance with requirements.
Other Instructions
**:** Industry Briefings:
1. 10:30am Wednesday 2 April 2025
2. 10:30am Thursday 3 April 2025
Both briefings will be held via Microsoft Teams
To register for briefings and additional information visit
New Builds Volume Leasing EOI

stage

**:** Stage 1 - Invitation to Register. Criteria: Desirability; and Feasibility.

Stage 2 - Request for Proposal. Criteria: Specific details regarding the Stage 2 evaluation process will only be released to Stage 1 respondents who have been invited to participate in Stage 2.
Description
**:** The Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator (ASCA) is transforming the way breakthrough military solutions are delivered by rapidly translating innovative technologies into capabilities that will help take Defence further, faster.
On Friday 1 December 2023, Defence Ministers announced the launch of an innovation challenge series for AUKUS Pillar II. The first of these challenges was focused on Electronic Warfare (EW). The second is focused on undersea Command, Control and Communications.
title: AUKUS Maritime Innovation Challenge 2025: Undersea Communications and Autonomy
cpv_descriptions: 72000000 - IT services: consulting, software development, Internet and support.

text:
llar II. The first of these challenges was focused on Electronic Warfare (EW). The second is focused on undersea Command, Control and Communications.
This is a trilaterally agreed challenge and will utilise a harmonised competition process with individual submissions entered into one of the three AUKUS partners’ individual submission portals (Australia, United Kingdom and United States). The challenge is being run as a two-stage competition across the Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator (ASCA) in Australia, the Defence and Security Accelerator (DASA) in the United Kingdom and the Defence Innovation Unit (DIU) in the United States.
You are invited to submit a response to Stage 1 of this competition via this this Invitation to Register (ITR) - ATM ID: AUKUS02-2025-ITR for the AUKUS Maritime Innovation Challenge 2025: Undersea Communications and Autonomy.
title: AUKUS Maritime Innovation Challenge 2025: Undersea Communications and Autonomy
cpv_descriptions: 72000000 - IT services: consulting, software development, Internet and support.

text:
this Invitation to Register (ITR) - ATM ID: AUKUS02-2025-ITR for the AUKUS Maritime Innovation Challenge 2025: Undersea Communications and Autonomy.
**AUKUS partners are seeking to research and develop innovations to enable the synchronisation and teaming of multiple undersea systems. We invite innovations that enable some or all of the following Desired Capability Effects:** 1. Provide near real time communications between Undersea Vehicles (UVs)
2. Provide near real time communications from UVs to Command and Control (C2) Systems /Battle Management System (BMS)
3. Provide near real time communications between seabed systems to UVs and C2 System and BMS
4. Provide a system that can optimally allocate the right asset to the right task in a dynamic and complex environment
5. Provide optimal bandwidth utilisation and effective range, and perform in a contested/congested environment
Other Instructions
title: AUKUS Maritime Innovation Challenge 2025: Undersea Communications and Autonomy
cpv_descriptions: 72000000 - IT services: consulting, software development, Internet and support.

text:
complex environment
5. Provide optimal bandwidth utilisation and effective range, and perform in a contested/congested environment
Other Instructions
**:** For full details and requirements, please refer to the AUKUS Maritime Innovation Challenge 2025: Undersea Communications and Autonomy documentation suite.
There will be a supporting launch Webinar run by each of the three AUKUS partners. In Australia, this is scheduled for 3 April 2025 at 1100 (AEDT). RSVP your interest at the link https://aukuschallenge25-au.anzpac.chime.live to confirm your attendance at the briefing. Please note there will be a 5 minute delay between registering and gaining access to the site.

text:
cturing of the 155mm M795 artillery projectile is planned to commence in 2028 under the Australian Guided Weapons and Explosives Ordnance (GWEO) Plan. At full capacity, the Australian forge facility in Benalla will produce up to 15,000 rounds per annum (current scope); with the capacity to surge to 100,000 with 12 months' notice.
The Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator (ASCA) is requesting quotations from participants to demonstrate their ability to domestically produce HF-1 steel that meets the MIL-S-70703 and MIL-S-50783 standards and exhibits the required grain structure and mechanical properties, in accordance with US standards.
Respondents must indicate their ability to produce HF-1 steel at an industrial scale, to meet the requirement of 900 tonnes of HF-1 steel bar per annum, with the ability to increase to 4,520 tonnes per annum with a 12-month lead time.

title: Industry Notice for Australian Defence Contracted Health Service
cpv_descriptions: 85100000 - Health services.

text:
een issued as a precursor to an upcoming multi-stage procurement process, with a Request for Proposal (RFP) anticipated for release in Quarter 3 2025. The RFP is intended to seek industry feedback and solutions with regards to a potential operating and delivery model for the provision of health care services to the Australian Defence Force (ADF). The RFP will also be used to assess the market’s capability and capacity to deliver against the full scope of Defence’s requirements, with a limited number of potential providers then being invited to participate in the next stage through a Request for Tender (RFT) process.
This notice is not a procurement. Any procurement process initiated by Defence in respect of the requirements will be released separately. Rather, this notice is designed to provide advance notification to industry of the intent to conduct a multi-stage procurement process.
title: Industry Notice for Australian Defence Contracted Health Service
cpv_descriptions: 85100000 - Health services.

text:
ed separately. Rather, this notice is designed to provide advance notification to industry of the intent to conduct a multi-stage procurement process. This notice does not guarantee that any future approach to market will actually be undertaken (nor about the form or timing of any such approach), and similarly does not represent a guarantee of works in any format.
Defence is seeking solutions to deliver comprehensive acute and chronic care services, both on and off base, that provide equitable access to health care irrespective of location, and includes optimisation of support to diverse populations and force elements. Proposals must complement and integrate with the existing ADF and Australian Public Service (APS) health workforce working within Joint Health Command facilities.
The procurement approach will recognise three distinct types of healthcare service requriemetns (scheduled, unscheduled and preventive), shaped by the nature of demand.
title: Industry Notice for Australian Defence Contracted Health Service
cpv_descriptions: 85100000 - Health services.

text:
proach will recognise three distinct types of healthcare service requriemetns (scheduled, unscheduled and preventive), shaped by the nature of demand. Scheduled and unscheduled care services are characterised by variable and sometimes unpredicatbale demand. This services are likely best delivered through a fee-for-service model, where providers are paid based on actual service use. Preventative care, including immunisations and occupational and clinical screening, is generally structured and predictabe. For these services, a fixed price model is proposed, with the built in ability to manage suges in personnel numbers or other schedued campaigns.
Data sets will form part of the RFP package to assist respondents to understand current service delivery demands, as well as constraints. RFP response will be used to inform the finalisation of a draft Statement of Work and Contracting Suite that is intended to be released to shortlisted tenderers via RFT in due course.
Other Instructions

text:
Market sounding will provide supporting evidence to inform the recommended option to Government for delivering and funding the infrastructure program. The Market Sounding is designed to evaluate the private sector's willingness and capability to invest (in the form of either debt or equity), construct, and manage various components of the program. This process involves engaging with potential investors, debt financiers, Defence contractors, and other relevant stakeholders to ascertain their interest, capacity, and expertise in contributing to the program.

Description
**:** Navy is the largest training provider in Australia for the maritime technical domain. Training Force is responsible for providing the training of both permanent and reserve Navy personnel from initial recruitment to ongoing employment training (e.g.
title: Large Shiphandling and Small Vessel Training
cpv_descriptions: 80640000 - Training and simulation in warships.

text:
e is responsible for providing the training of both permanent and reserve Navy personnel from initial recruitment to ongoing employment training (e.g. trade/profession/platform specific) including professional career development, in the most efficient and effective way.
**Some deficiencies have been identified which, due to a number of factors, Training Force is unable to deliver the appropriate courses and/or proficiencies and to address the subsequent skill gaps. The Commonwealth is seeking to engage a highly capable and experienced contractor to develop and deliver tailored courses that align specifically with the Navy’s requirements, specifically:** a. Deliver vessel master training for vessels up to 24 metres in length to the Australian Industry Standard of a “Master up to 24m Near Coastal” for up to 36 personnel per year. This training is to include RAN specific content relating to the operation of vessels such as Steber craft, RAN Dive launches and MDREU support vessels.
title: Large Shiphandling and Small Vessel Training
cpv_descriptions: 80640000 - Training and simulation in warships.

text:
is training is to include RAN specific content relating to the operation of vessels such as Steber craft, RAN Dive launches and MDREU support vessels. Practical elements of the course are to be provided in Sydney, NSW and Rockingham, WA. The contractor shall provide the training platform for Sydney-based training and the RAN will provide the training platform for WA-based training. The Sydney-based platform should represent RAN Small vessels in size and construction as best as possible. Training may be delivered over a number of courses throughout the year.
b. One large shiphandling training course for deep specialist navigators of RAN deep draught vessels over 10 000 tonne for up to 8 personnel per year. Training is to include berthing and unberthing, slow speed manoeuvring, use of tug assets in shiphandling including tug orders and commands and include the option for azipod specific shiphandling training.
title: Large Shiphandling and Small Vessel Training
cpv_descriptions: 80640000 - Training and simulation in warships.

text:
eed manoeuvring, use of tug assets in shiphandling including tug orders and commands and include the option for azipod specific shiphandling training. Themes should include forces and their effects, natural elements and their effects, UKC effects, turning theory, propulsion train types and momentum in shiphandling. The course is to include simulator-based training and classroom theory.
c. Two large shiphandling training courses for Command of deep draught-vessels over 10 000 tonne for up to 5 personnel per course per year. Training is to include execution and oversight of berthing and unberthing, slow speed manoeuvring, use of tug assets in shiphandling and include the option for azipod specific shiphandling training. Themes should include forces and their effects, natural elements and their effects and momentum in shiphandling. The course is to include simulator-based training and classroom theory.
d.
title: Large Shiphandling and Small Vessel Training
cpv_descriptions: 80640000 - Training and simulation in warships.

text:
r effects, natural elements and their effects and momentum in shiphandling. The course is to include simulator-based training and classroom theory.
d. Two GMDSS courses for the award of AMSA approved General Operator’s Certificate for up to 12 people per course per year.

title: RAIL TRANSPORT STANDING OFFER FOR SERVICES
cpv_descriptions: 60200000 - Railway transport services.; 60210000 - Public transport services by railways.; 63711000 - Support services for railway transport.

text:
ng to establish a Panel to provide the Commonwealth of Australia with additional and surge capacity and capability for the movement of cargo by rail.
Strategic requirements to support annual or biennial military exercises may necessitate an increase in the amount of cargo to be transported in a single movement. Contractors engaged under this Panel would be tasked with the movement of Australian Deference Force and other Commonwealth stores and equipment by rail. This may include complex and/or short-notice tasking requirements. The transportation of personnel over large distance to support exercises and operations may also be required.

Description
**:** Logistics Assurance Branch (LAB) seeks to engage an Outsourced Service Provider (OSP) delivering specialist statistical analysis services for the purposes of logistical assurance sampling, specifically the execution of the National Asset and Inventory Sample (NAIS) and Military Support Items Impairment Sample (IS) from July 2025.
title: ENGAGEMENT OF STATISTICAL SUPPORT FOR DEFENCE
cpv_descriptions: 72000000 - IT services: consulting, software development, Internet and support.

text:
pling, specifically the execution of the National Asset and Inventory Sample (NAIS) and Military Support Items Impairment Sample (IS) from July 2025.
In order to certify the statistical methodology and results over an organisation as large and complex as Defence, the OSP must have at least one person classified as an expert in the field of statistical analysis. The proposed engagement will have deliverable outcomes including the annual methodology, analysis plans and final outcomes report. It will be for a three-year period commencing from FY 2025-26, with two additional 12 months  extension options, subject to Key Performance Indicators and deliverables being achieved. This will allow for consistency in adapting and refining the methodologies as ERP emerges and reaches maturation in sustainment.

Description
**:** The National Defence Strategy (NDS) has directed that the Australian Army ‘optimise for littoral manoeuvre with a long-range land and maritime strike capability’.
title: LAND8710 PH5 Littoral Manoeuvre Program – Facilities
cpv_descriptions: 98300000 - Miscellaneous services.

text:
Defence Strategy (NDS) has directed that the Australian Army ‘optimise for littoral manoeuvre with a long-range land and maritime strike capability’. The Government has prioritised the acquisition of new littoral manoeuvre capabilities and infrastructure to support achievement of the NDS. The establishment of the littoral manoeuvre capability is a vital step in the restructure and re-posture of the Integrated Force. It will ensure that the Australian Defence Force (ADF) will continue to play a vital role in responding to our strategic challenges.
In alignment with NDS priorities, Defence will acquire new littoral manoeuvre capabilities through an investment of $7 to $10 billion in littoral manoeuvre vessels and $5 to $7 billion in related facilities. The Commonwealth of Australia (Commonwealth) as represented by the Department of Defence (Defence) is currently developing LAND8710 Phase 5 – Littoral Manoeuvre Program – Facilities (the Project).
title: LAND8710 PH5 Littoral Manoeuvre Program – Facilities
cpv_descriptions: 98300000 - Miscellaneous services.

text:
represented by the Department of Defence (Defence) is currently developing LAND8710 Phase 5 – Littoral Manoeuvre Program – Facilities (the Project).
The aim of the Project is to deliver the facilities required to meet the Littoral Manoeuvre Program capability requirements in locations including Southeast Queensland (SQ), Northern Queensland (NQ) and the Northern Territory (NT). The Project scope includes delivery of maritime facilities (including, berthing, mooring and vessel loading / unloading) and landside facilities (including office accommodation, living-in accommodation, homeporting facilities, training facilities and other base support functions) in each location.
The Commonwealth is currently undertaking Development Phase activities for this Project. Key activities in this phase include development of the Master Plan Feasibility Report (MPFR) and Preliminary Detailed Business Case (PDBC), which will help inform recommended procurement and delivery strategies for the Project.
title: LAND8710 PH5 Littoral Manoeuvre Program – Facilities
cpv_descriptions: 98300000 - Miscellaneous services.

text:
Report (MPFR) and Preliminary Detailed Business Case (PDBC), which will help inform recommended procurement and delivery strategies for the Project.
The Commonwealth wishes to inform the market that a future procurement may be undertaken in relation to this Project through this Notice.

text:
ulti Agency Access

Description
**:** Airfield -
The community airfield features an unsealed surface, characterised by low pavement strength, insufficient drainage, and several minor shortcomings. The northern section of the runway is prone to flooding, which reduces its usable length by at least 500 meters. Additionally, the lighting is inadequate, restricting runway operations to daylight hours only. Consequently, the airfield cannot be used during or immediately after rainfall, and past flooding of the Fitzroy River has further hindered its use. This situation adversely affects casualty evacuations, essential deliveries such as food and medical supplies, and routine travel. The proposed scope includes upgrading the airfield to facilitate all-weather access at any time of day or night, along with enhancements to the surrounding airfield area.
title: Design Services Consultant-Airfield Upgrade and Shop Design
cpv_descriptions: 34928470 - Signage.; 34960000 - Airport equipment.; 45235110 - Construction work for airfields.

text:
cludes upgrading the airfield to facilitate all-weather access at any time of day or night, along with enhancements to the surrounding airfield area.
The runway is expected to undergo widening and lengthening, accompanied by enhanced drainage systems. Crucially, it will be elevated to ensure it is above flood levels. A key aspect of this upgrade is to preserve the capability for landing Royal Flying Doctors Service aircraft, as well as potentially accommodating military fixed-wing aircraft for humanitarian assistance. Notably, the runway will not need CASA certification but will meet the minimum length and width stipulated by 19 CE Wks on behalf of the client
and Army
in accordance with  Civil Aviation Safety Regulations (CASR) 19 Regulation
To meet the military physical requirement, the airfield needs to be designed in accordance with CASR part 139, Code 3D. This requirement will be reviewed at the 30% design stage once indicative costs for the whole project are known.
title: Design Services Consultant-Airfield Upgrade and Shop Design
cpv_descriptions: 34928470 - Signage.; 34960000 - Airport equipment.; 45235110 - Construction work for airfields.

text:
cordance with CASR part 139, Code 3D. This requirement will be reviewed at the 30% design stage once indicative costs for the whole project are known. Key to this decision is the ability to use the community quarry at no cost to the project against importing from a commercial quarry.  The minimum length and width will be stipulated by 19 CE Wks on behalf of the Client.
Community Shop -
The Yungngora Community shop is currently managed by Outback Stores on behalf of the Noonkanbah Aboriginal Corporation (NAC). However, it lacks the necessary capacity to adequately address the food security requirements of the Community and is unable to adequately serve the smaller neighbouring communities in wet season. Establishing a new shop will provide the needed capacity for Community supply during the wet season and enhance its overall functionality. The design must take into account the potential for flooding at the chosen site and incorporate measures to mitigate this risk.
title: Design Services Consultant-Airfield Upgrade and Shop Design
cpv_descriptions: 34928470 - Signage.; 34960000 - Airport equipment.; 45235110 - Construction work for airfields.

text:
verall functionality. The design must take into account the potential for flooding at the chosen site and incorporate measures to mitigate this risk.
Geotechnical Testing.
**The Consultant is to conduct all geotechnical testing required to complete designs. Additionally, the Consultant is required to provide the following results in a report as soon as practicable to inform the Contract Administrators cost estimations:** Aggregate sampling and testing of quarry material to sufficiently inform suitability as an engineered material in construction of airfield pavement layer.
Water source testing for construction quality assurance purposes prior to incorporating them into concrete and earthworks.

Description
**:** This Notice provides information on the current status of ESTJ2092PH1 (JP2092PH1) Guided Weapons Explosive Ordnance Enterprise Program – Program of Works and Defence's current intention in relation to upcoming Design Services procurement.
Project Scope
title: ESTJ2092 PH1 (JP2092 PH1) Guided Weapons Explosive Ordnance Enterprise Program – Procurement Notice (Design Services)
cpv_descriptions: 35300000 - Weapons, ammunition and associated parts.

text:
ive Ordnance Enterprise Program – Program of Works and Defence's current intention in relation to upcoming Design Services procurement.
Project Scope
The JP2092PH1 Guided Weapons Explosive Ordnance Enterprise Program – Program of Works (National) will support the Government’s direction to increase the Guided Weapons and Explosive Ordnance capability throughout Australia.  The investment in Guided Weapons and Explosive Ordnance (GWEO) is a key 2024 National Defence Strategy (NDS) initiative and requires additional investment in the Storage & Distribution (S&D) network to deliver a strategically aligned and fit-for-purpose network.
**The Project includes following Work Elements (WE) and associated scope elements:** WE 1.  Mount Stuart Training Area (QLD) – expand existing deep storage node,
WE 2.  Townsville Field Training Area (QLD) – New Surge Facility Storage and Transfer Hub (SATH),
WE 3.  Twofold Bay (NSW) – expand existing deep storage node,
title: ESTJ2092 PH1 (JP2092 PH1) Guided Weapons Explosive Ordnance Enterprise Program – Procurement Notice (Design Services)
cpv_descriptions: 35300000 - Weapons, ammunition and associated parts.

text:
nsville Field Training Area (QLD) – New Surge Facility Storage and Transfer Hub (SATH),
WE 3.  Twofold Bay (NSW) – expand existing deep storage node,
WE 4.  RAAF Base Gingin (WA) – new deep storage node,
WE 5.  Cultana Training Area (SA) – new deep storage node,
WE 6.  Woomera Test Range (SA) - new deep storage node,
WE 7.  King River (NT) – new Surge Facility Storage and Transfer Hub (SATH),
WE 8.  RAAF Base Learmonth (WA) - new Surge Facility Storage and Transfer Hub (SATH).
Potential tenderers are advised that the procurement strategy for the ESTJ2092PH1 Project involves the engagement of a Design Services Consultant (DSC) to provide design services, via Invitation to Register Interest and subsequently Request for Tender (RFT) released to shortlisted tenderers.
Tenderers will be required to demonstrate their capacity to design large scale and complex GWEO facilities and infrastructure located in often remote and climatically challenging conditions, as part of their ITR response.
title: ESTJ2092 PH1 (JP2092 PH1) Guided Weapons Explosive Ordnance Enterprise Program – Procurement Notice (Design Services)
cpv_descriptions: 35300000 - Weapons, ammunition and associated parts.

text:
scale and complex GWEO facilities and infrastructure located in often remote and climatically challenging conditions, as part of their ITR response.
The ESTJ2092PH1 Project value is estimated at $1.85B.
Procurement Opportunities
This Notice is for the purposes of informing Industry that the Project will be releasing approaches to market for the provision of design services.
It is anticipated that the Design Services Contract  -  Invitation to Register Interest (ITR)will be released in Q3 2025 on AusTender (https:// www.tenders.gov.au).
This is a Notice of an upcoming approach to market only. Prospective tenderers are not entitled to make any claim for, and the Commonwealth shall not be liable for, any liability, costs or loss incurred as a result of any delay in the release of the ITR.

Following the release of the 2024 National Defence Strategy (NDS), updated Integrated Investment Program (IIP) and Defence Industry Development Strategy (DIDS), Space and Cyber Capabilities Division (SCCD) will host the SCCD ‘Capable and Sustainable’ - Cyber Domain Classified Industry Forum.
title: SCCD'Capable and Sustainable'Cyber Domain Classified Industry Forum
cpv_descriptions: 75220000 - Defence services.

text:
Strategy (DIDS), Space and Cyber Capabilities Division (SCCD) will host the SCCD ‘Capable and Sustainable’ - Cyber Domain Classified Industry Forum.
Thank you for your interest in attending ‘Capable and Sustainable’ – Cyber Domain. This forum is open to Defence Industry that have a current or future interest in supporting Defence in delivering Cyber capabilities.
‘Capable and Sustainable’ - Cyber Domain Forum will be held on Monday 18 August 2025 at Catalina House, Majura Park, ACT.
Registrations close 16:30 Friday 25 July 2025, to allow time for the vetting and feedback on the nominations. Nomination outcomes will be advised by Friday 04 August 2025.
The final program will be provided closer to the event.
You are required to complete a form to register for attendance at ‘Capable and Sustainable’ - Cyber Domain  Forum. Nominations for industry attendance are restricted to two representatives per company.
Event Overview
title: SCCD'Capable and Sustainable'Cyber Domain Classified Industry Forum
cpv_descriptions: 75220000 - Defence services.

text:
Capable and Sustainable’ - Cyber Domain  Forum. Nominations for industry attendance are restricted to two representatives per company.
Event Overview
The purpose of this forum is to provide an overview of our threat environment, our challenges and our strategic intent and priorities for future capability development. The ‘Capable and Sustainable' - Cyber Domain Forum will maintain a constructive dialogue with industry and provide an avenue for Defence Industry and Academia, to share their views on broader opportunities and challenges. Defence subject matter experts will discuss the current and future capabilities and pressures to give industry the opportunity to understand where they can engage, invest and contribute to delivery of Cyber capabilities.
The discussion will be held at the Secret (NV1) level. All personnel in attendance will require an active NV1 or higher security clearance.
Each organisation may nominate up to two attendees, in priority / preferred order to attend.

Each organisation may nominate up to two attendees, in priority / preferred order to attend. We will confirm your attendance by 04 August 2025 via email. text:
nel Arrangement

Multi-stage

Description
**:** *********Invitation to participate in a Test and Evaluation Industry Webinar ********
The Defence Industry Development Strategy and the accompanying Sovereign Defence Industry Priority 7 - Test, Evaluation, Certification and Systems Assurance were released in 2024.  Ahead of their release, Industry had submitted welcome feedback during 2022 and 2023 on possible industry partnership models and had contributed generously to analysis of the Test and Evaluation Workforce and Infrastructure.
The Defence Test and Evaluation Strategy remains active and Horizon 2 is well underway.  Nevertheless, there is an obligation to check it remains aligned with the newer Strategy and Priority documents and that work has commenced.
title: Test and Evaluation Industry Briefing Session
cpv_descriptions: 79419000 - Evaluation consultancy services.

text:
derway.  Nevertheless, there is an obligation to check it remains aligned with the newer Strategy and Priority documents and that work has commenced.
Defence wishes to encourage initiatives and investment in Test, Evaluation, Certification and Systems Assurance by cooperating more closely with industry.  It is clear that an innovative, resilient and competitive defence industrial base creates a stronger and more secure Australia.
The subject webinar will include a briefing on planned engagements with industry during FY2025-2026 through which Defence will implement its vision for better cooperation.
The webinar will be held on 8th May from 1330 to 1415, which includes time for a Q&A panel.
Please register your interest: https://tecsa.anzpac.chime.live/

ndertake a two-stage approach for Mission Syracuse.

"How might we provide viable sovereign effector options for the ADF Integrated Force Capability optimised to mitigate the threats posed by small UAS?"
For the purpose of this RFP, ‘sovereign’ is defined as a capability that the Commonwealth of Australia can access at a time of need including when international trade may be disrupted.

Description
**:** Background
The GIDP is the primary operational and support base for the Royal Australian Navy (RAN) on Australia’s eastern coast. The Project aims to enable maritime operational effectiveness and improve base operations through the remediation of strategic infrastructure and accommodation of future capability requirements and support workforce.  The GIDP is comprised of the "Main Site" and several supporting satellite states, including the Chowder Bay DFI(M). The remediation of aging facilities and infrastructure in the GIDP is a key priority.
Project Scope
**Of the satellite sites, the Chowder Bay DFI(M) (the subject of this notice), has been identified as key to meeting the RAN’s minimum fuel reserve requirements for the Sydney region.  The works at this location will include:** a.       demolition of aged buildings that have exceeded their remaining useful life and that are no longer fit-for-purpose;
title: Building construction and support and maintenance and repair services
cpv_descriptions: 45210000 - Building construction work.

text:
location will include:** a.       demolition of aged buildings that have exceeded their remaining useful life and that are no longer fit-for-purpose;
b.       redevelopment of larger buildings with flexible spaces, which may include the upgrade of support services, to efficiently deliver a variety of base functions; and
c.       fuel infrastructure upgrades and sustainment of strategic fuel storage for the east coast maritime capability, including the replacement of existing marine diesel fuel tanks and associated vessel offloading and dispensing (bunkering) infrastructure.
Capital Facilities and Infrastructure Branch (CFI) is responsible for developing and delivering capital facilities and infrastructure projects across the Defence estate, including this Project.
At the time of this notice, the estimated value of the works is between $150 million and $170 million (GST exclusive).
Procurement Opportunities

Procurement Opportunities
This
notice
is for the purposes of informing industry that Defence intends to conduct a two-stage procurement process to engage two Early Contractor Involvement Head Contract (ECI-HC) Contractors, using the Defence Suite of Facilities Contracts ECI-HC - Two Contractor Planning Phase Approach templates.  Two ECI-HC Contractors are expected to be engaged to support design development and planning of the works in the Project’s Planning Phase followed by the anticipated selection of one of the Contractors to continue to deliver the works on a construct only basis in the Project’s Delivery Phase.
Defence anticipates releasing an Invitation to Register (ITR) as the first stage of the procurement process in mid- to late-August 2025 on AusTender (https://www.tenders.gov.au).

**For this call to market, ASCA is looking for cutting-edge, innovative proposals to apply one or more of the following technology areas to the Opportunity Statements for Decision Advantage:** Automated Data Integration & Interpretation;
Machine Reasoning;
Multi-Modal Data Fusion;
Augmented Human-Machine Reasoning;
Artificial Intelligence (AI) Agents;

comprehensive array of initial, intermediate and advanced courses for all categories within the Maritime Logistics, Personnel and Health communities.

Description
**:** EST02146 National Maritime Infrastructure Maintenance Program (NMIMP) aims to identify and remediate identified deficiencies in specific maritime structures requiring major capital investment to maximise their useful life.
**The project will be delivered across the following Defence establishments:** ·         Fleet Base West – HMAS Stirling (Western Australia);
·         Fleet Base East – Garden Island Defence Precinct (GIDP) (New South Wales);
·         HMAS Coonawarra (Northern Territory);
·         Harold E Holt (HEH) Defence Precinct (Western Australia); and
·         Twofold Bay Ammunition Wharf (TBAW) (New South Wales),
The Works may include structural remediation, corrosion protection, pavement upgrades, and the reinstatement of critical maritime infrastructure elements.
title: EST02146-National Maritime Infrastructure Maintenance Program (NMIMP) – Design Services Consultant (DSC) – Invitation
cpv_descriptions: 22314000 - Designs.; 71230000 - Organisation of architectural design contests.; 79311100 - Survey design services.

text:
Works may include structural remediation, corrosion protection, pavement upgrades, and the reinstatement of critical maritime infrastructure elements. This could involve activities such as pile wrapping and repairs, cathodic protection system upgrades, pavement and deck rehabilitation, and the restoration of mooring and berthing systems. In some locations, further investigation will be required to confirm site conditions, assess the extent of deterioration, and inform the Consultant’s deliverables in the Development Phase.

title: ESTJ2092 PH1 (JP2092 PH1) Guided Weapons Explosive Ordnance Enterprise Program-DSC Invitation to Register
cpv_descriptions: 48451000 - Enterprise resource planning software package.

text:
in and sustaining Australia’s domestic capability to increase and improve the supply surety, availability and reliability of the ADF’s GWEO inventory. The investment in Guided Weapons and Explosive Ordnance (GWEO) is a key 2024 National Defence Strategy (NDS) initiative and requires additional investment in the Storage & Distribution (S&D) network to deliver a strategically aligned and fit-for-purpose network. The location of the Project is Nationwide. The following Work Elements (WE) have been identified:
WE 1.  Mount Stuart Training Area (QLD) – expand existing deep storage node,
WE 2.  Townsville Field Training Area (QLD) – New Surge Facility Storage and Transfer Hub (SATH),
WE 3.  Twofold Bay (NSW) – expand existing deep storage node,
WE 4.  RAAF Base Gingin (WA) – new deep storage node,
WE 5.  Cultana Training Area (SA) – new deep storage node,
WE 6.  Woomera Test Range (SA) - new deep storage node,
WE 7.  King River (NT) – new Surge Facility Storage and Transfer Hub (SATH),

text:
Australia, as represented by the Department of Defence, is seeking to establish a Panel to provide domestic and international Air Transport services.
Strategic requirements under this Panel are likely to be in support of military Exercises, Operations and Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief within Australia and overseas. Services required under the Panel are anticipated to include provision of aircraft and movement of cargo and/or passengers.
A request for tender for the Air Transport Standing Offer for Services is anticipated to be released through AusTender in mid-late August 2025, or shortly thereafter.
Prospective tenderers are encouraged to review their notification settings in AusTender to ensure notification of further updates in relation to the RFT.
This notice, and the information contained within, supersedes the pre-notification to industry previously published on 27 May 2025.

Description
**:** Dear Australian Industry and Academia
The Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator (ASCA) is transforming the way breakthrough military solutions are delivered by rapidly translating innovative technologies into capabilities that will help take Defence further, faster.
title: ASCA Pitch Day 2025
cpv_descriptions: 79952000 - Event services.

text:
through military solutions are delivered by rapidly translating innovative technologies into capabilities that will help take Defence further, faster. To support this initiative, ASCA is excited to host its second pitch day. As outlined in the Defence Industry Development Strategy, pitch days will allow industry and academia to present their ideas to Defence, allowing for insights into new capabilities and innovations.
This year’s iteration, ASCA Pitch Day 2025, is a Request For Information (RFI) on solutions to Challenge Statements grouped into two themes: Power and Energy and Acoustic Data Collection and Processing. Power and Energy (Theme 1) seeks innovative solutions on energy sources for deployable and fixed sites, and agile operation capabilities. Acoustic Data Collection and Processing (Theme 2) seeks innovative solutions on sensors, edge processing and high-performance computing for data processing.
title: ASCA Pitch Day 2025
cpv_descriptions: 79952000 - Event services.

text:
c Data Collection and Processing (Theme 2) seeks innovative solutions on sensors, edge processing and high-performance computing for data processing.
ASCA invites you to respond to this open RFI by submitting your innovative solution(s) in accordance with the Conditions of RFI. Respondents that submit the most promising solutions, as determined by Defence’s subject matter experts, will be invited to pitch at the Pitch Day 2025 event on 3 November 2025 in Sydney, NSW, coinciding with the Indo-Pacific International Maritime Exposition (4 - 6 November 2025). Subject to the Conditions of RFI, ASCA will arrange and fund travel and accommodation for successful respondents’ attendance at the Pitch Day 2025 event. These successful respondents will be pitching to an audience comprising senior Defence officials, potential investors and wider Defence industry and academia.

Description
**:** Consistent with the Defence Industry Development Strategy and the One Defence Capability System, Navy recognises the importance of communicating our capability needs, as well as building a stronger relationship with defence industry earlier in the capability’s life cycle.
title: Classified Maritime Domain Industry Forum November 2025
cpv_descriptions: 60651600 - Offshore supply ship services.; 63726400 - Ship chartering services.; 76522000 - Offshore supply vessel services.

text:
ance of communicating our capability needs, as well as building a stronger relationship with defence industry earlier in the capability’s life cycle.
To this end, Rear Admiral Stephen Hughes, Head Navy Capability, will be holding a classified Maritime Domain Industry Forum on Friday 7 November 2025 in Sydney after INDO PACIFIC 2025. The aim of the forum is for industry to gain a better understanding of Navy’s capability priorities and information on when and where future industry opportunities may be available.
Other Instructions
**:** Due to the limited capacity of the secure venue, nominations are restricted to two representatives per organisation. In the event that the number of expressions of interest exceeds the venue capacity: (a) priority will be given to those businesses deemed to best represent their respective industry sector and (b) the number of representatives will be reduced to one per organisation.
title: Classified Maritime Domain Industry Forum November 2025
cpv_descriptions: 60651600 - Offshore supply ship services.; 63726400 - Ship chartering services.; 76522000 - Offshore supply vessel services.

text:
e businesses deemed to best represent their respective industry sector and (b) the number of representatives will be reduced to one per organisation.
The discussion will be held at the SECRET level, therefore, this event is only open to individuals who already hold a current Australian Government Vetting Agency (AGSVA) security clearance of Negative Vetting 1 (NV1) or higher.
All expressions of interest will be reviewed and vetted to ensure attendees are eligible to attend and their business interests align with Navy's immediate industrial capability outcomes.
Conditions for Participation
**:** The classified Maritime Domain Industry Forum is open to businesses, with an Australian-based industrial capability and an Australian Business Number, that have a current or future interest in supporting Defence and Navy in delivering Maritime Domain capabilities.
title: Classified Maritime Domain Industry Forum November 2025
cpv_descriptions: 60651600 - Offshore supply ship services.; 63726400 - Ship chartering services.; 76522000 - Offshore supply vessel services.

and an Australian Business Number, that have a current or future interest in supporting Defence and Navy in delivering Maritime Domain capabilities.
This event is only open to individuals who already hold a current Australian Government Vetting Agency (AGSVA) security clearance of Negative Vetting 1 (NV1) or higher.
The Commonwealth reserves the right to determine who can participate at its sole discretion.
The Commonwealth will not bear any costs associated with attending this forum.

**:** The LC4 IS&T Summit will be held in Canberra on the 02-03 October 2025. This event will contribute to the Strategy of Denial by identifying critical Science and Technology (S&T) capabilities and opportunities across the Integrated C4 (IC4) ecosystem that are most relevant to the Land Joint Force. The summit will prioritize initiatives necessary for the land component of the Future Integrated Force.

at are most relevant to the Land Joint Force. The summit will prioritize initiatives necessary for the land component of the Future Integrated Force.
This initiative aligns with Defence’s strategic objective of accelerating technological adoption and strengthening industry collaboration. It will be facilitated through engagement and co-design with senior leaders across Defence’s Integrated C4 domains.
Other Instructions
**:** Both days of the LC4 IS&T Summit will have classified briefings and will be only open to individuals who hold a Negative Vetting 1 Security Clearance or higher. Note, capacity is limited to two persons people per Company. Registrations will be confirmed via email once security clearances have been confirmed.

Description
**:** The LC4 Program seeks innovative, interoperable, and scalable solutions to strengthen the Australian Defence Force’s communications, information networks, and decision-making capabilities in highly contested and degraded tactical environments.
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge
cpv_descriptions: 32424000 - Network infrastructure.; 72212219 - Miscellaneous networking software development services.; 72720000 - Wide area network services.

text: tralian Defence Force’s communications, information networks, and decision-making capabilities in highly contested and degraded tactical environments. Across three key focus areas, Defence aims to: (1) enhance network resilience by developing self-healing, adaptive, and secure communications that can operate under persistent electronic warfare and cyber threats; (2) modernise tactical communications to enable seamless, secure, low-latency exchange of voice, data, and video across dismounted, mounted, and headquarters units, ensuring integration with coalition partners; and (3) deliver decision advantage at the tactical edge by harnessing AI, machine learning, advanced edge computing, and adaptive ISR to support real-time data fusion and mission-tailored visualisation in austere conditions.
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge
cpv_descriptions: 32424000 - Network infrastructure.; 72212219 - Miscellaneous networking software development services.; 72720000 - Wide area network services.

text:
machine learning, advanced edge computing, and adaptive ISR to support real-time data fusion and mission-tailored visualisation in austere conditions. Industry is invited to propose technologies that address challenges such as resilient mesh networking, anti-jamming, spectrum agility, quantum-resilient cryptography, software-defined radios, 5G/6G-enabled systems, lightweight edge communications, human-machine teaming, and distributed sensor integration to support future-ready, multi-domain operations.
This multi-domain approach supports Defence’s commitment to accelerating capability delivery, enhancing interoperability with allies, and strengthening Australia’s sovereign industrial base.
2. STATEMENT OF DEFENCE NEEDS
Focus Area 1: Enhancing Network Resilience in Contested Environments
**Challenge Statement:** Defence seeks innovative solutions to enhance the resilience of communications and information networks operating in highly contested and degraded tactical environments.
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge

text:
ative solutions to enhance the resilience of communications and information networks operating in highly contested and degraded tactical environments. Proposed technologies must enable mission assurance through self-healing, adaptive routing, multi-path redundancy, low probability of detection/interception (LPD/LPI), and the ability to function under persistent electronic warfare and cyber threat conditions.
**Key Focus Areas:** • Resilient mesh networking and dynamic routing protocols
• Anti-jamming and spectrum agility tools
• Quantum-resilient cryptography
• Autonomous detection and response to network compromise
• Integration of SATCOM, terrestrial, and near-space communications for redundancy next generation networks, next generation bearers and PNT alternatives
Focus Area 2: Modernisation of Tactical Communications
**Challenge Statement:** Defence is exploring solutions to modernise tactical communication systems for dismounted, mounted, and headquarter units.

ns
**Challenge Statement:** Defence is exploring solutions to modernise tactical communication systems for dismounted, mounted, and headquarter units. The aim is to ensure seamless, secure, low-latency voice, data, and video exchange in bandwidth-constrained and mobile tactical environments. Industry is invited to propose interoperable and scalable communication technologies that support multi-domain operations and integration with coalition partners.
**Key Focus Areas:** • Software-defined radios (SDRs) with modular, reprogrammable waveforms
• 5G/6G-enabled tactical communication solutions
• Lightweight and ruggedized edge communications kits
• Multilevel secure communications (MLS) for coalition interoperability
• Unified communications platforms with real-time translation and media-sharing

Focus Area 3: Decision Advantage at the Tactical Edge in Contested Environments
**Challenge Statement:** Defence is seeking innovative capabilities that enable commanders and warfighters at the tactical edge to gain decision advantage despite data overload, contested information environments, and limited connectivity. Solutions should support real-time data fusion, local AI/ML processing, and intuitive decision support in austere conditions.
**Key Focus Areas:** • AI-powered situational awareness and predictive analytics at the edge
• Edge compute devices for local data processing and inference
• Human-machine augmentation at the tactical edge.
• Distributed sensor integration and adaptive ISR platforms
• User-centric interfaces and mission-tailored visualisation tools
• Next generation networks, next generation bearers and PNT alternatives

title: ASCA-Undersea Navigation Challenge (UNC)
cpv_descriptions: 45221246 - Undersea tunnel construction work.; 71351924 - Underwater exploration services.; 76121000 - Subsea well diving services.

text:
bilities Accelerator (ASCA), within the Department of Defence, invites you to respond to the Undersea Navigation Challenge Request for Proposal (RFP). ASCA seeks to identify and contract suitably qualified and experienced industry partners to develop a Prototype Undersea Navigation System (UNS) that can be matured through a phased development. If you are evaluated as successful you will be invited to enter contract negotiations with a view to enter into a prototype development and demonstration contract with ASCA for a duration of up to 18 months.
Specifically, under this Challenge ASCA seeks proposals for the development of a low Size, Weight, Power and Cost (SWAP-C) navigation system for autonomous undersea vehicles that can provide improved accuracy over traditional inertial navigation systems without aiding from satellite position systems or acoustic transmissions.
title: ASCA-Undersea Navigation Challenge (UNC)
cpv_descriptions: 45221246 - Undersea tunnel construction work.; 71351924 - Underwater exploration services.; 76121000 - Subsea well diving services.

text:
t can provide improved accuracy over traditional inertial navigation systems without aiding from satellite position systems or acoustic transmissions. ASCA is seeking a comprehensive proposal on how your organisation intends to develop and test the proposed technology, including a detailed budget and project execution plan. The Commonwealth envisages that solutions could exploit geophysical references such as magnetic or gravitational anomaly maps, but is open to other technology solutions.
Funding available for this Challenge is limited to $3.85 million (including GST) to fund up to 2 technology development contracts.

text:
he home ownership assistance provided to members of the Australian Defence Force (ADF) through the Defence Home Ownership Assistance  Scheme (DHOAS).
The Defence Home Ownership Assistance Scheme (DHOAS) is established under the Defence Home Ownership Assistance Scheme Act 2008 (Cth) (DHOAS Act) to provide Australian Defence Force (ADF) members with assistance to achieve home ownership.
The DHOAS forms one element of the Australian Government's initiative to increase ADF recruitment and retention.
The scheme is administered on behalf of the Commonwealth by the Department of Veterans’ Affairs.

Description
**:** Training Authority Maritime Warfare (TA-MW) is the principal warfare training authority of the Royal Australian Navy (RAN). TA-MW provides warfare training for all rank levels from Seaman to Captain via a wide variety of courses ranging from one week to two years.
title: Performance Coaching Services
cpv_descriptions: 34510000 - Ships.; 79998000 - Coaching services.; 80500000 - Training services.

text:
vy (RAN). TA-MW provides warfare training for all rank levels from Seaman to Captain via a wide variety of courses ranging from one week to two years. These courses provide instruction in basic and advanced warfare (procedural and tactical).
Warfare training is generally very complex and trainees can experience a variety of psychological stress. Trainees may be impacted by pressure, fatigue and psychological stress as a result of complex and stressful situations. Thus, TA-MW requires an industry partner to assist and facilitate the delivery of this element of training, Performance Coaching.
Performance Coaching assists trainees cope with pressure and identify their strengths and areas for improvement in order to achieve their goals. It helps them identify their specific development needs and create a tailored development plan to address them.
title: Performance Coaching Services
cpv_descriptions: 34510000 - Ships.; 79998000 - Coaching services.; 80500000 - Training services.

text:
ement in order to achieve their goals. It helps them identify their specific development needs and create a tailored development plan to address them. This individualised attention is the key advantage of performance coaching over other development methods such as workshops, which often provide generalised information that is not relevant to the individual.
TA-MW is located within HMAS Watson; however, schools are located at various Defence establishments. Suitable facilities and training systems exist at HMAS Watson, HMAS Stirling, HMAS Cerberus, HMAS Penguin, HMAS Waterhen and other Defence sites to facilitate delivery of training.

Description
**:** The LC4 Program seeks innovative, interoperable, and scalable solutions to strengthen the Australian Defence Force’s communications, information networks, and decision-making capabilities in highly contested and degraded tactical environments.
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge
cpv_descriptions: 30238000 - Library automation equipment.; 72212211 - Platform interconnectivity software development services.; 72228000 - Hardware integration consultancy services.

text: tralian Defence Force’s communications, information networks, and decision-making capabilities in highly contested and degraded tactical environments. Across three key focus areas, Defence aims to: (1) enhance network resilience by developing self-healing, adaptive, and secure communications that can operate under persistent electronic warfare and cyber threats; (2) modernise tactical communications to enable seamless, secure, low-latency exchange of voice, data, and video across dismounted, mounted, and headquarters units, ensuring integration with coalition partners; and (3) deliver decision advantage at the tactical edge by harnessing AI, machine learning, advanced edge computing, and adaptive ISR to support real-time data fusion and mission-tailored visualisation in austere conditions.
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge
cpv_descriptions: 30238000 - Library automation equipment.; 72212211 - Platform interconnectivity software development services.; 72228000 - Hardware integration consultancy services.

text:
machine learning, advanced edge computing, and adaptive ISR to support real-time data fusion and mission-tailored visualisation in austere conditions. Industry is invited to propose technologies that address challenges such as resilient mesh networking, anti-jamming, spectrum agility, quantum-resilient cryptography, software-defined radios, 5G/6G-enabled systems, lightweight edge communications, human-machine teaming, and distributed sensor integration to support future-ready, multi-domain operations.
This multi-domain approach supports Defence’s commitment to accelerating capability delivery, enhancing interoperability with allies, and strengthening Australia’s sovereign industrial base.
The LC4 Program will utilise the information and insights provided through this RFI process to inform the development of future submissions and capability proposals. Formal feedback will be provided to companies in due course upon completion of the RFI process.
STATEMENT OF DEFENCE NEEDS
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge
cpv_descriptions: 30238000 - Library automation equipment.; 72212211 - Platform interconnectivity software development services.; 72228000 - Hardware integration consultancy services.

text:
and capability proposals. Formal feedback will be provided to companies in due course upon completion of the RFI process.
STATEMENT OF DEFENCE NEEDS
Focus Area 1: Enhancing Network Resilience in Contested Environments
**Challenge Statement:** Defence seeks innovative solutions to enhance the resilience of communications and information networks operating in highly contested and degraded tactical environments. Proposed technologies must enable mission assurance through self-healing, adaptive routing, multi-path redundancy, low probability of detection/interception (LPD/LPI), and the ability to function under persistent electronic warfare and cyber threat conditions.
**Key Focus Areas:** • Resilient mesh networking and dynamic routing protocols
• Anti-jamming and spectrum agility tools
• Quantum-resilient cryptography
• Autonomous detection and response to network compromise
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge
cpv_descriptions: 30238000 - Library automation equipment.; 72212211 - Platform interconnectivity software development services.; 72228000 - Hardware integration consultancy services.

text:
outing protocols
• Anti-jamming and spectrum agility tools
• Quantum-resilient cryptography
• Autonomous detection and response to network compromise
• Integration of SATCOM, terrestrial, and near-space communications for redundancy next generation networks, next generation bearers and PNT alternatives
Focus Area 2: Modernisation of Tactical Communications
**Challenge Statement:** Defence is exploring solutions to modernise tactical communication systems for dismounted, mounted, and headquarter units. The aim is to ensure seamless, secure, low-latency voice, data, and video exchange in bandwidth-constrained and mobile tactical environments. Industry is invited to propose interoperable and scalable communication technologies that support multi-domain operations and integration with coalition partners.
**Key Focus Areas:** • Software-defined radios (SDRs) with modular, reprogrammable waveforms
• 5G/6G-enabled tactical communication solutions
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge
cpv_descriptions: 30238000 - Library automation equipment.; 72212211 - Platform interconnectivity software development services.; 72228000 - Hardware integration consultancy services.

text:
rtners.
**Key Focus Areas:** • Software-defined radios (SDRs) with modular, reprogrammable waveforms
• 5G/6G-enabled tactical communication solutions
• Lightweight and ruggedized edge communications kits
• Multilevel secure communications (MLS) for coalition interoperability
• Unified communications platforms with real-time translation and media-sharing
Broadcast Communications
Focus Area 3: Decision Advantage at the Tactical Edge in Contested Environments
**Challenge Statement:** Defence is seeking innovative capabilities that enable commanders and warfighters at the tactical edge to gain decision advantage despite data overload, contested information environments, and limited connectivity. Solutions should support real-time data fusion, local AI/ML processing, and intuitive decision support in austere conditions.
**Key Focus Areas:** • AI-powered situational awareness and predictive analytics at the edge
• Edge compute devices for local data processing and inference
title: Land 4140 Submission 3 Capability Challenge
cpv_descriptions: 30238000 - Library automation equipment.; 72212211 - Platform interconnectivity software development services.; 72228000 - Hardware integration consultancy services.

text:
Focus Areas:** • AI-powered situational awareness and predictive analytics at the edge
• Edge compute devices for local data processing and inference
• Human-machine augmentation at the tactical edge.
• Distributed sensor integration and adaptive ISR platforms
• User-centric interfaces and mission-tailored visualisation tools
• Next generation networks, next generation bearers and PNT alternatives

Description
**:** Defence Housing Australia (DHA) is issuing this Notice of Intended Procurement (NOIP) for our upcoming Townsville - QLD Aitkenvale Dwelling Relocation Expression of Interest (EOI).
Through this opportunity we are seeking proposals from industry to relocate and reuse vacant dwellings located in Aitkenvale.
The EOI provides an opportunity for suitability qualified organisations to provide proposals to purchase, relocate and reuse/renovate up to 68 vacant post-war era dwellings located at adjacent addresses in Aitkenvale, Townsville, as part of a planned redevelopment program.  The dwellings are available for reuse subject to strict compliance with all relevant statutory, licencing and safety requirements.

from any subsequent process to this EOI, DHA expects all participants in that process to satisfactorily comply with the conditions for participation. By submitting a response, the successful Applicant acknowledges they are aware they will be required to enter into an appropriate Deed or Contract, on terms acceptable to DHA, requiring the Applicant to (among other things) remove the dwellings from the site within a specified period of time following the settlement of sale of the dwellings.
Description
**:** Defence Housing Australia (DHA) is seeking expressions of interest (EOI) for the purchase and relocation of 68 existing dwellings located in Aitkenvale, Townsville, QLD. The vacant dwellings are required to be removed from the site as part of a planned redevelopment program.
title: Expression of Interest-Dwelling Relocation in Townsville, QLD
cpv_descriptions: 45215210 - Construction work for subsidised residential accommodation.; 70333000 - Housing services.; 75123000 - Administrative housing services.

text:
ngs located in Aitkenvale, Townsville, QLD. The vacant dwellings are required to be removed from the site as part of a planned redevelopment program.
The dwellings are all three-bedroom, one-bathroom residential homes constructed between 1965 and 1969. The dwellings are constructed of asbestos cement sheeting, and timber and concrete posts.
The dwellings will be sold “as is, where is” or in other words, exactly as they are in their current form. The successful Applicant will be required to enter into an appropriate Deed or Contract, on terms acceptable to DHA, requiring the Applicant to (among other things) remove the dwellings from the site within a specified period of time following the settlement of sale of the dwellings.
The dwellings are known to contain asbestos and other contaminants. The asbestos material identified on site is considered non friable in nature.

Description
**:** The Director General Systems & Integration (DG S&I) has formally released the updated Land Network Integration Centre (LNIC) Strategic Plan, outlining a refined approach to digital integration, technical assurance, and capability modernisation across the Land Combat System (LCS). This strategy sets the direction for LNIC’s transformation over the next five years, aligning with Army’s priorities and the broader objectives of the Integrated Force.
**This Industry Day will provide Defence industry partners with:** - An overview of the LNIC Strategic Plan, including its vision, mission, and seven strategic goals designed to assure the digital integration of Army’s capabilities.

the LNIC Strategic Plan, including its vision, mission, and seven strategic goals designed to assure the digital integration of Army’s capabilities.
- An opportunity to understand the role of LNIC, including its support to Army, Joint, and Coalition partners in delivering integrated, interoperable, and cyber-assured capabilities.Insights into LNIC’s evolving engagement model, including its use of Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE), digital standards, and risk-reduction experimentation through federated Battle Labs and System Integration Labs (SILs).
- An understanding of LNIC’s status as an Accredited Engineering Organisation (AEO) and Test & Evaluation Unit (T&E ), reinforcing its authority and accountability in delivering validated, technically assured integration outcomes.

Industry participation is critical to achieving the LNIC’s mission to deliver a digitally integrated, protected, connected, and lethal Land Combat System. This event will foster transparency, collaboration, and shared understanding of integration requirements and opportunities.

Description
**:** The Australian Army History Unit (AAHU) is seeking a partnership with a service provider for publishing services, with a high level of academic credibility, for the publication of the Australian Army History Series (AAHS). Titles in the AAHS are academically rigorous books that explore a topic, subject or theme in a manner in which it has either not been discussed before, or sheds new understandings on an existing topic that drives scholarly inquiry further and makes a new contribution to the field.
AAHU are seeking a partnership for the publication of the series whereby copies of each publication are provided to AAHU for their use and distribution while the publishing provider is able to market the publication to meet their own commercial requirements.
title: Academic Publishing-Army History Series
cpv_descriptions: 22120000 - Publications.

text:
ded to AAHU for their use and distribution while the publishing provider is able to market the publication to meet their own commercial requirements.
AAHU will attract authors and will provide developed proposals and draft manuscripts to the publisher for inclusion in the AAHS. The publisher should also encourage potential authors for the AAHS and may direct potential projects to AAHU. While the publisher will be contracted to publish up to three works annually, there will be no minimum or maximum limit to the number of Works that may be submitted.
The titles in the AAHS must be high quality hardback publications, fully referenced with endnotes and bibliography, include all necessary frontmatter and a quality index. The average publication size of AAHS titles is 100,000 words and will include images, maps, tables and diagrams in colour and/or black and white. AAHU and the author will provide images and maps to the publisher, including associated permissions.
title: Academic Publishing-Army History Series
cpv_descriptions: 22120000 - Publications.

text:
es and diagrams in colour and/or black and white. AAHU and the author will provide images and maps to the publisher, including associated permissions. The publisher, based on input from AAHU and the author, will produce tables and diagrams, and should be able to offer cartographic services.
**An esteemed Australian military historian will be engaged by AAHU as a General Series Editor for the AAHS to work with authors and undertake reviews of draft manuscripts. Appropriate peer-reviews are expected to be undertaken by the publisher. The publisher and AAHU will work collaboratively to ensure manuscripts meet the standards of both AAHU, as the client, and the publisher. The publisher will be responsible for copyediting. AAHU will require the publisher to provide academically rigorous publishing services to a standard agreed with the AAHU, including but not limited to the following:** -Copy-editing by an experienced military history editor
-Production, including design and typesetting
title: Academic Publishing-Army History Series
cpv_descriptions: 22120000 - Publications.

text:
, including but not limited to the following:** -Copy-editing by an experienced military history editor
-Production, including design and typesetting
-Proofreading
-Indexing
-Printing
-Publication
-Marketing, and
-Distribution.
The publisher will be required to develop a consistent design format for AAHS titles, in collaboration with AAHU. AAHU will be required to approve the design and final proofs of all titles. All titles will be required to include reference to the AAHU, the AAHS, the Series Editor, and include on the front cover and spine the Rising Sun Badge in accordance with Army branding requirements.
The publisher will be required to attend at minimum, quarterly production meetings. The publisher will be required to attend at minimum, quarterly marketing and sales meetings.
title: Academic Publishing-Army History Series
cpv_descriptions: 22120000 - Publications.

text:
ired to attend at minimum, quarterly production meetings. The publisher will be required to attend at minimum, quarterly marketing and sales meetings. The publisher is to provide a marketing plan for each project, using traditional and electronic media that considers the widest dissemination of the publications, in all formats for the lowest possible retail cost, commensurate with commercial imperatives.
The Commonwealth will underwrite all Works commissioned by the AAHU at an agreed rate per Work. The publisher shall bear all the other costs of publishing and promoting the Work. The recommended retail price of the books in the AAHS shall be at the discretion of the publisher, provided there is prior reasonable consultation between the publisher and AAHU.
AAHU expects a minimum print run of 1000 copies of each title published in the AAHS.
title: Academic Publishing-Army History Series
cpv_descriptions: 22120000 - Publications.

text:
is prior reasonable consultation between the publisher and AAHU.
AAHU expects a minimum print run of 1000 copies of each title published in the AAHS. 300 copies of each publication will be delivered to AAHU for their use and distribution, and 700 copies of each publication to be marketed by the publisher to meet their commercial requirements. The publisher will provide 10 advance copies of each title at no cost to the AAHU in Canberra. The publisher must also publish an e-book version, and provide access to AAHU at no cost.
**A pricing schedule should be submitted in response to these requirements. This should include:** -Subvention fee per AAHS title
-Cost of 300 copies at a discount rate
-Pricing schedule for additional copies purchased at discounted rates
-Pricing schedule for remaindering stock.
The publisher should also provide detail confirming that additional works, including special book projects, will be considered.
