Location
**:** Other
ATM Type
**:** Request for Information
Multi Agency Access
**:** No
Panel Arrangement
**:** No
Multi-stage
**:** No
Description
**:** The Department of Defence (Defence) has an ongoing and increasing demand for data for Defence and National Security intelligence purposes. A key data element is what is commonly referred to as Intelligence Mission Data (IMD), which is used to support the reprogramming of military platforms and systems. Defence has a current and continuing need to increase the volume, speed and fidelity of IMD production and it is anticipated that Industry support will be critical to achieving this aim.
title: Intelligence Mission Data (IMD) Industry Discussion Paper
cpv_descriptions: 72312100 - Data preparation services.; 72319000 - Data supply services.; 72322000 - Data management services.

text:
need to increase the volume, speed and fidelity of IMD production and it is anticipated that Industry support will be critical to achieving this aim.
The purpose of this Paper is to obtain a better understanding of the market’s current and future capability and capacity in data management skills which are key to the delivery of IMD services.  Defence is also interested in exploring innovative approaches and contracting models to support the growth of industry capability and capacity to align with the growing Defence demand.
This Paper has been published to facilitate a more collaborative relationship with industry, to initiate discussion and obtain feedback from industry that will inform a potential future procurement process.

Description
**:** Run Army requires the services of an Event Management Team. The contractor will be responsible for timing and merchandise services for all Run Army events scheduled for 2027 and possibly future years. This includes the physical events in Brisbane and Townsville and any satellite and virtual events scheduled.
The contractor will provide expected budget requirements as agreed upon by the Run Army Directorate. The contractor will provide key staff to support the administering of the event, including race timing services, bibs, timing chips, race pack collection, advertising, capacity planning and third party sponsorship arrangements.
The contractor will also provide support for media, including iPad device for race commentator, live tracking, social media support and advertising. The contractor will organise and facilitate event operations within the approved budget.

Description
**:** The Joint Transition Authority (JTA) will be conducting an open Request for Information (RFI) to help Defence determine the most suitable ICT platform(s), and ancillary/support services, to deliver Transition Seminars. This RFI will also aid in determining the best method to procure the identified platforms(s).
The JTA requires an 'Events Management' software platform to host and manage the Seminars. The JTA also requires ancillary/support services essential to the successful running of the software, including technical expertise before and during the Seminars to troubleshoot platform faults and issues. These services are a critical part of the requirement and form an essential part of running live Seminars and maintaining the availability of static (not live) Seminars.

Description
**:** Defence estates across New South Wales and the Australian Capital Territory comprise a wide range of facilities that depend on natural gas for essential services, including heating, cooking, and hot water. Maintaining a secure and reliable gas supply is critical to ensuring operational continuity and cost efficiency across these sites.
This Request for Tender seeks to establish a contract for the supply of natural gas to Defence’s large market sites across New South Wales and the Australian Capital Territory via existing gas network infrastructure.

Description
**:** The Australian Government, through Defence and supported by other relevant agencies, is seeking proposals from Australia's private capital market to inform its potential investment into an Advanced Capabilities Investment (ACI) Fund together with private capital.
The Australian Government may consider a co-investment of up to $500 million into one or more ACI Fund(s) to finance Australian companies involved in the development of defence and dual-use advanced capabilities.
The purpose of an ACI Fund(s) will be to invest in Australian small to medium sized businesses alongside private capital to support the development of kinetic and kinetic-enhancing advanced defence capabilities, including dual-use capabilities, with a view to growing and scaling such businesses, including for potential export opportunities
title: Request for Expressions of Interest-Advanced Capabilities Investment (ACI) Fund
cpv_descriptions: 66122000 - Corporate finance and venture capital services.; 66140000 - Portfolio management services.

text:
fence capabilities, including dual-use capabilities, with a view to growing and scaling such businesses, including for potential export opportunities
Through this Request for Expressions of Interest (REOI), Defence seeks submissions from interested parties to identify potential Private Capital Partner(s) to establish and manage an ACI Fund(s).
Respondents should ensure they carefully read and understand all requirements of this REOI and exercise independent skill and judgement when interpreting the document and preparing their response.
**The REOI pack comprises:** - Conditions of REOI | ACI Fund
- Schedule 1 – Glossary, interpretation and precedence
- Schedule 2 – ACI Parameters
- Schedule 3 – Deed of undertaking
- Schedule 4 – Respondent Submission Form
Other Instructions
**:** An optional online industry briefing will be held from 15:30 to 16:30 (Canberra, Australian Capital Territory local time) on 12 March 2026.

Description
**:** The Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator’s (ASCA) mission is to accelerate the development and transition of asymmetric capabilities to the Australian Defence Force through innovation, in order to meet Defence priority needs.
title: Advance Notice-ASCA Pitch Day 2026-Resilient Command and Control
cpv_descriptions: 79952000 - Event services.; 79952100 - Cultural event organisation services.; 79953000 - Festival organisation services.

text:
he development and transition of asymmetric capabilities to the Australian Defence Force through innovation, in order to meet Defence priority needs.
ASCA's Pitch Days provide industry and academia the opportunity to present their ideas to Defence, ensuring Defence remains aware of new capabilities and innovations.
This advance notice is for the first of two Pitch Days in 2026, which will be a Request For Information (RFI) on new capabilities and innovations that ensure resilient command and control (C2). C2 refers to the process and means for the exercise of authority over, and lawful direction of, assigned forces.
Submissions describing such capabilities and innovations—dubbed 'solutions'—must address Challenge Statements that fall under this theme of 'Resilient C2'.
title: Advance Notice-ASCA Pitch Day 2026-Resilient Command and Control
cpv_descriptions: 79952000 - Event services.; 79952100 - Cultural event organisation services.; 79953000 - Festival organisation services.

text:
sions describing such capabilities and innovations—dubbed 'solutions'—must address Challenge Statements that fall under this theme of 'Resilient C2'.
Respondents to the RFI that submit promising solutions (as assessed by a panel of Defence subject matter experts) will be invited to pitch to an audience of senior Defence officials, other valued guests and members of the broader defence ecosystem at the Pitch Day event, to be hosted at the Australian Defence Science, Technology and Research Summit (ADSTAR) 2026 at Adelaide, in August.
Further information on this upcoming RFI, including the Challenge Statements, assessment criteria and Pitch Day event, will be made available from March 2026.
**Visit the ASCA website:** www.asca.gov.au/opportunities
Other Instructions
**:** This is a notice for an upcoming Request For Information only. The decision to submit a response is a matter for your company. You are under no obligation to respond and any response is submitted on a voluntary basis.

Description
**:** The LC4 IS&T Summit 2026 is a two-day event scheduled for 27-28 May 2026 in Adelaide, South Australia. The summit will support Defence’s Strategy of Denial by identifying and accelerating the adoption of emerging Science and Technology (S&T) capabilities critical to the Integrated C4 (IC4) ecosystem.
title: LC4 ISand TSummit 2026
cpv_descriptions: 79952100 - Cultural event organisation services.; 79953000 - Festival organisation services.

text:
nial by identifying and accelerating the adoption of emerging Science and Technology (S&T) capabilities critical to the Integrated C4 (IC4) ecosystem. It will focus on technologies that enhance the agility, resilience, and lethality of the Land Joint Force within the Future Integrated Force, including quantum sensing, edge AI, autonomous systems, resilient communications, and battlefield data fusion. The program will feature an update on the LAND4140 Program, panel discussions with Defence and key stakeholders, and a fireside chat with senior leadership. The summit will also explore operational integration of emerging technologies in contested environments, strengthen collaboration with Australian industry and academia, and drive innovation through co-design with Defence leaders. This initiative aligns with Defence’s strategic objectives to rapidly adopt technology, enhance sovereign capability, and foster industry partnerships.
Other Instructions
title: LC4 ISand TSummit 2026
cpv_descriptions: 79952100 - Cultural event organisation services.; 79953000 - Festival organisation services.

text:
s with Defence’s strategic objectives to rapidly adopt technology, enhance sovereign capability, and foster industry partnerships.
Other Instructions
**:** Both days of the LC4 IS&T Summit will have classified briefings and will be only open to individuals who hold a Negative Vetting 1 Security Clearance or higher. Note, capacity is limited to two persons people Company. Registrations will be confirmed by 24th April via email.
Conditions for Participation
**:** The LC4 IS&T Summit is open to Australian companies, with an ABN, that have a current or future interest in supporting Defence in the Land C4 space. Attendance is limited to two attendees per organisation. Registrations will be vetted to ensure the conditions of participation are adhered to.
